howItWorks.intro: This reading level calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Higher ease score = easier to read (60-70 is standard, 80+ is easy)
howItWorks.outro: Educational costs and financial aid vary by institution. Contact schools directly for current figures.

faq.question: What is a good Flesch-Kincaid score?
faq.answer: For general audiences, aim for grade level 7-8 and a Reading Ease score of 60-70. Most newspapers write at grade 7-8. Academic papers score grade 12+. Web content performs best at grade 6-8.

faq.question: How do I count syllables?
faq.answer: Count vowel sounds in each word. 'Cat' = 1, 'happy' = 2, 'beautiful' = 3, 'understanding' = 4. Silent 'e' doesn't count. Adjacent vowels making one sound ('boat') count as one. Use online syllable counters for large texts.

faq.question: How can I improve readability?
faq.answer: Use shorter sentences (15-20 words average). Choose simpler words when possible. Break long paragraphs into shorter ones. Use active voice. Avoid jargon unless necessary for your audience. Aim for one idea per sentence.

howWeCalculate.intro: This reading level calculator uses established formulas and current data to provide accurate estimates.

howWeCalculate.disclaimer: These calculations are estimates based on standard formulas. For critical decisions, always consult a qualified professional.
